gate_mode: change_gate
gate_claim: the guard-hardening repair introduces no security regression: every git invocation stays argument-array and pathspec-scoped to the actual archive dest with no shell interpolation of branch, ref, or path names, the new base validation cannot be spoofed by a crafted branch name, a symbolic ref, a detached HEAD, or a falsified base_branch field, the post-commit re-resolution and reachability check trusts only git's own refs and never operator-controlled durable state, the treeDirty, parked-lane, foreign-dirt, and never-mutate-sibling protections are not weakened, and no secrets or new trust of operator-controlled input are introduced across all four edition copies
gate_surface: the full epoch-3 claim.js delta in all four editions (guard validation, post-commit re-verification, both call sites, restore-gate interplay) plus its interaction with the epoch-1 sink-preflight exemption surface, attacked for injection, path-traversal, staging-scope, ref-spoofing, and guard-weakening primitives
gate_aggregation: sequence
upstream_read: n3-code-certify-hardening 212afaa15a1f

verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
review_summary: no_blocking_findings
review_attestation: full_review_completed

## Scope and method

- Candidate = the 14-file accumulated worktree diff vs claim root base cd28e8e52fb641cf2173ced57c91a042e3c13e1e in .kw/worktrees/issue-715 (verified: git rev-parse HEAD == cd28e8e5, matching the review context). git status --porcelain shows exactly the 14 in-set modified files plus the untracked kaola-workflow/issue-715/ run tree: zero out-of-set writes.
- Security surface examined in full: the epoch-3 claim.js delta (treeDirty third parameter :537-573, the hardened commitDiscardArchive helper :2364-2469, the cmdRelease restoreExempt + releaseBaseBranch hoist + post-restore helper call + emit disclosures :3422/:3430-3447/:3464-3465/:3490-3492/:3496-3499, the cmdWatchPr sweep pre-read + defaultBase + guarded call + cleanup-entry disclosures :4430-4438/:4452-4453/:4492-4495, exports :4790-4793) and its interaction with the epoch-1 sink-preflight exemption hunk in the four sink-merge.js copies.
- Upstream honored as evidence, not authority: n3-code-certify-hardening (nonce 212afaa15a1f matches its line-1 binding) read in full; the epoch-2 n5 refutation (7e6482a523a9), n1 producer evidence (f6077183028a), and my epoch-2 predecessor certification (87ac23d2764e, lineage only) all read in full. Every adjudication independently re-derived from the diff and by my own probes.
- Role honored strictly read-only: no repository file created, modified, or deleted. Verification was Read/Grep/git diff plus my own probe suite (38 assertions) built under $TMPDIR/kw715-n4-probes driving the REAL shipped helper via require() against throwaway tmp repos, plus re-execution of the shipped suites (which fixture under os.tmpdir() and clean up).

## Injection audit — CLOSED

- Every git invocation in the shipped-script delta is execFileSync with an argument array: helper rev-parse --show-toplevel, rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD (guard and post-commit re-resolution), rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<base>, add -A -- <rel>, diff --cached --quiet -- <rel>, commit -m <msg> -- <rel>, merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD base, cat-file -t HEAD:<rel>; treeDirty status with -uall appended as a literal array element; restore checkout. grep of all added lines in all 8 shipped files (4 claim.js + 4 sink-merge.js) finds zero exec/spawn/eval/new Function/shell:true additions (count 0 per file). The spawnSync additions exist only in the test-only surfaces (simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js, test-claim-hardening.js), all argument-array form driving fixtures.
- Branch names, base, project, and dest appear only as single argv elements or in string equality — never concatenated into a command string. The commit message embeds folder.project as one argv element; my P2 probe (project = 'p$(touch <tmpfile>)') confirms no shell ever sees it: no file created, commit still truthful.
- rev-parse --verify takes 'refs/heads/' + base as ONE argv element: even a base beginning with '-' cannot be option-parsed there (the string starts with 'r'), and the refs/heads/ prefix confines the lookup to the branch namespace.
- merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD base takes base as one argv element: my P11 probe created a real local branch named '-evil' (via update-ref) and checked it out (symbolic-ref); the helper staged and committed onto it (the operator's actual checkout, equal to base — a legitimate bind), and the merge-base probe then failed CLOSED (git rejects '-evil' as an unknown option, nonzero exit caught, committed:false with a truthful detail). A dash-leading base can never produce option injection (argv array, no shell) and never a false committed:true.

## Ref-spoofing and base-validation audit — CLOSED

Every falsified-base class is refused before staging, at both call sites (the guard lives inside the helper, so both inherit it):
- Detached-HEAD sentinel: clause (a) rejects base === 'HEAD' outright (:2389-2393). Probe P3 (detached, base 'HEAD'): committed:false, branch 'HEAD' disclosed, tip unchanged, no chore commit on any ref. Probe P4: a whitespace-padded '  HEAD  ' is trimmed and refused identically.
- Crafted branch names: clause (b) requires a real local branch via argument-array rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<base> (:2394-2406). P6: a tag lookalike base (tag v9 exists, no branch) refused with 'does not name a real local branch'. P7: the full-ref form 'refs/heads/main' refused (verify looks up refs/heads/refs/heads/main). P5: the revsyntax base 'main^{commit}' passes the verify but is refused by the retained epoch-2 equality guard (:2423-2427) — rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD never prints revsyntax.
- Symbolic refs: P15 created refs/heads/sym as a symref to refs/heads/main and pointed HEAD at it; git resolves abbrev-ref HEAD through the chain to 'main', so base 'sym' mismatches the equality guard and is refused — fail-closed, no spoof.
- Falsified base_branch naming the discarded branch: clause (c) (:2407-2413) refuses base === opts.discardedBranch; the release passes the feature branch it KNOWS it discards (:3465). P8: refused with 'names the branch being discarded', feature tip byte-unchanged.
- Falsified base_branch naming the current arbitrary lane at the sweep: clause (d) (:2414-2422) requires base === opts.defaultBase at the posture with no restore step. P9: refused with 'not the surviving default branch', BOTH ref tips byte-unchanged, no chore commit anywhere.
- The precondition honestly stated by n5 stands: both claim paths clamp baseBranch to '' for 'HEAD'/self, so the tooling never writes the falsified values — a hand-edit or external corruption remains required, and within that precondition every demonstrated cell is now refused.

## Refusal ordering — cannot be bypassed

All five guard clauses (non-empty, sentinel, real-branch verify, discardedBranch, defaultBase) plus the epoch-2 equality check sit BEFORE the first mutating call (git add, :2430) in the helper body; there is no path from guard entry to staging that skips a clause (sequential ifs, each returning on refusal). Proven behaviorally, not just by reading: in every refusal probe (P3/P4/P5/P6/P8/P9) git status --porcelain afterwards shows the dest still fully untracked ('?? kaola-workflow/' — no add ran) and for-each-ref tips are byte-unchanged, and git log --all contains no 'chore: discard archive' subject. The shipped hardening pins assert the same pre-staging property; test-claim-hardening.js re-ran by this reviewer: 485 assertions passed.

## Trust derivation of post-commit checks — CLOSED

After the commit, the helper consults ONLY git's own refs: branchAfter from rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD (:2446), reachability from merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD base (:2454-2461, fail-closed on ANY probe error), and the retained presence check cat-file -t HEAD:<rel> (:2462-2464). No operator-controlled durable state (workflow-state files, receipts, ledgers) is read anywhere post-commit — the only durable input (baseBranch) is consumed by the pre-staging guard and then re-proven against live refs. My P16 probe (deterministic PATH git shim interposing at the helper's add: real add, then symbolic-ref HEAD onto race): the helper committed onto race, then the re-resolution saw branchAfter='race' !== 'main' and DOWNGRADED to { committed: false, branch: 'race' } — disclosing the ACTUAL receiver, never the stale pre-race base; main tip byte-unchanged, the archive a tree at race (recoverable), not a tree at main. The shipped end-to-end pin (testReleaseHeadRepointRaceDowngradesArchiveCommit) re-ran green standalone by this reviewer with the explicit never-emit-'main' assertion.

## Pathspec/staging-scope audit — CLOSED

- rel is derived from the dest's own rev-parse --show-toplevel and path.relative; an empty rel (dest == toplevel) or any rel starting with '..' is refused before any git mutation (:2349-2358). P12 (dest outside the repo): refused, repo refs and porcelain untouched. P13 (dest == toplevel): refused. P14 (dest with '..' segments normalizing inside the repo): no false report, no chore commit for an empty dir.
- Every mutating pathspec is preceded by a literal '--' separator (add/diff/commit), so a dest-relative path beginning with '-' is not an option primitive.
- Staging scope cannot be widened past the dest: P10 pre-staged a foreign change (foreign.txt) BEFORE invoking the helper; the helper's commit (git commit -- <rel>) touched ONLY kaola-workflow/archive/<dest> paths (verified via git show --name-only) and left the foreign change still staged (A  foreign.txt) — git commit with a pathspec takes working-tree content under the pathspec only and cannot sweep foreign staged changes elsewhere.
- No new fs write primitives anywhere in the claim.js delta (grep for writeFileSync/appendFileSync/mkdirSync/renameSync/cpSync/copyFileSync/unlinkSync/rmSync additions: none) — the only writes are the git add/commit scoped to rel. The archive move itself is pre-existing archiveProjectDirSafely behavior (epoch-1, untouched by this delta).

## Guard-preservation audit — INTACT

- treeDirty (:537-573): the exemption is segment-boundary exact (norm === e || norm.startsWith(e + '/')) with symmetric normalization on both sides; -uall is appended ONLY when an exemption is non-empty (so a collapsed untracked tree cannot hide the exempt dest OR a foreign sibling beside it); the catch path still returns true (fail closed). The executable (comment-stripped) body is md5-identical in canonical/gitlab/gitea (ee5dc02a82e2de765007321007717996) — the raw diff across trees is comments only.
- The two other treeDirty callers (:1158, :1353) pass no exemption and are behavior-unchanged; only cmdRelease passes restoreExempt, derived from the ACTUAL result.dest with the same '..' rejection (:3430-3436).
- isParkedLanePath semantics untouched (the only functional edit adds the !isExempt(p) conjunct; the parked-lane call is byte-identical); kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js (never-mutate-sibling / archive never-parked) is absent from the candidate diff entirely.
- Epoch-1 sink-preflight exemption surface: the sink-merge.js delta vs HEAD is exactly the epoch-1 18 +/- lines (SINK_RECEIPT_EXEMPT regex, byte-identical in all four copies, md5 0c57acf433b9e4275e551ce78d4d159f of the regex line); no epoch-3 hunks. The anchored single-segment regex ^kaola-workflow/(?:archive/)?[^/]+/\.cache/sink-receipt\.json$ has no prefix or glob escape and deliberately does NOT exempt .discarded-* residue — an uncommitted archive stays bucket-3 foreign dirt that loudly blocks the next sink, matching the new warnings[] text on the skip path. test-sink-merge.js re-ran by this reviewer: 105 assertions passed including the (l) over-exemption guard and (m) own-receipt regression lock.
- Restore-gate interplay: the exemption admits exactly the one release-created dest; foreign sibling dirt beside it still blocks the restore (the helper then truthfully skips with residue recoverable) — covered by the re-run 485-assertion suite pins and the sibling-receipt walkthrough cell.

## Four-edition parity — PROVEN, not inferred

- Codex twin: diff scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js EMPTY; same for sink-merge.js — byte-identical.
- commitDiscardArchive body md5 c767f927efd3f6c507ae159194b4bcf2 re-computed by this reviewer over function commitDiscardArchive...} in canonical, gitlab, and gitea — identical in all three. Exactly 3 commitDiscardArchive( occurrences per forge file (1 definition + 2 call sites: gitlab :2104/:3182/:3612, gitea :2103/:3177/:3607) — no missed or extra site.
- Call-site hunks mirrored in both forge ports: releaseBaseBranch hoist + restoreExempt at the dirty gate (gitlab :3154, gitea :3149) + call with discardedBranch only (gitlab :3183, gitea :3178); sweep pre-read + sweepDefaultBase + call with both opts (gitlab :3613, gitea :3608); emit disclosures; exports (gitlab :4490, gitea :4482).
- node scripts/validate-script-sync.js -> OK (25 common scripts, 28 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families, 7 forge export-superset families in sync); node scripts/edition-sync.js --check -> parity (12 forge aggregator ports, 25 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, 28 byte-identical groups).

## Probe results (own, against the REAL shipped helper via require(), fixtures under $TMPDIR only)

- 38/38 passed ($TMPDIR/kw715-n4-probes/probe.js): P1 honest on-base commit with tree at main and reachability proven; P2 shell-metachar project name inert; P3/P4 detached sentinel and padded sentinel refused pre-staging with tip unchanged; P5 revsyntax base refused by equality; P6 tag lookalike refused at verify; P7 full-ref form refused; P8 discarded-branch base refused; P9 arbitrary-lane base refused at the sweep with both tips unchanged; P10 foreign pre-staged change not swept (commit touches only the dest, foreign change remains staged); P11 dash-leading branch name: no option injection, reachability probe fails closed, disclosure verifiable; P12 out-of-repo dest refused; P13 toplevel dest refused; P14 normalized in-repo dest never false-reports; P15 symref base resolves through and fails closed on equality; P16 HEAD-repoint race downgrades to committed:false disclosing the ACTUAL receiver 'race' (never stale 'main'), main tip unchanged, commit recoverable on race; P17 helper body contains zero OFFLINE branches (a commit that runs is never skipped for offline).
- Corroborating shipped-suite re-execution by this reviewer in the worktree: node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js -> 485 assertions passed; node scripts/test-sink-merge.js -> 105 assertions passed; simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js --only for each of testReleaseDetachedHeadLyingBaseSkipsArchiveCommit, testReleaseOnFeatureBranchLyingBaseNamesDiscardedBranchSkips, testWatchPrClosedSweepDetachedLyingBaseHeadSkips, testWatchPrClosedSweepArbitraryLaneLyingBaseSkips, testReleaseHeadRepointRaceDowngradesArchiveCommit, testSinkForeignDirtExemptsSiblingReceipt715 -> each passed standalone.
- One residual worth recording (classification-only, non-blocking, matching n3's O-N3-1 family): under P11's exotic posture (a dash-leading branch the operator has actually checked out, equal to the recorded base) the helper commits legitimately onto that checkout and then conservatively reports committed:false because merge-base cannot parse the dash-leading name; the commit is recoverable on the disclosed branch. The failure direction is always closed — no false durability claim is reachable — and the precondition (a falsified-or-exotic dash branch name the tooling never writes, checked out by the operator) is outside any realistic threat path. Not a candidate-caused vulnerability.
- Secrets scan: no secret-shaped additions anywhere in the 14-file diff (key/token/credential/bearer/private-key patterns: none). Emitted detail strings disclose only branch names and repo-relative paths in operator-local JSON.

## Conclusion

Every attack vector in the gate surface was traced to code and, where attacker-reachable, executed against the real helper: no injection primitive exists (argv arrays throughout, '--'-separated pathspecs), the base validation refuses the sentinel, crafted names, revsyntax, tags, symrefs, discarded branches, and arbitrary lanes before staging with provable no-staging ordering, the post-commit checks derive trust only from git's own refs and fail closed, the pathspec cannot be widened and foreign staged changes cannot be swept, the treeDirty/parked-lane/foreign-dirt/never-mutate-sibling guards and the epoch-1 sink-preflight exemption are undiminished, and all four edition copies carry byte- or md5-proven identical guard semantics. Zero candidate-caused vulnerabilities found.

findings_none: true

domain_outcome: approved

review_conclusion: the epoch-3 guard-hardening introduces no security regression across all four edition copies: every git invocation stays execFileSync argument-array with '--'-separated pathspecs scoped to the actual result.dest and no shell interpolation of branch, ref, project, or path names (my probes show shell-metachar project names inert and dash-leading branch names fail closed with no option injection); the in-helper base validation refuses the detached-HEAD sentinel (including whitespace-padded forms), tag lookalikes, full-ref forms, revsyntax bases, symbolic refs, falsified discarded-branch and arbitrary-lane bases BEFORE any staging at both call sites (proven by still-untracked porcelain and byte-unchanged ref tips on every refusal); the post-commit re-resolution and merge-base reachability check trust only git's own refs and downgrade the HEAD-repoint race to a truthful committed:false disclosing the ACTUAL receiving branch, never the stale base; the '-- <rel>' pathspec cannot be widened by a crafted dest (out-of-repo, toplevel, and traversal forms all refused) and cannot sweep foreign staged changes (verified: pre-staged foreign file untouched by the helper's commit); treeDirty's segment-boundary exemption, the parked-lane, foreign-dirt, and never-mutate-sibling protections, and the anchored epoch-1 sink-preflight exemption are undiminished (sink-merge delta is epoch-1 only, regex byte-identical in all four copies, never exempting discard residue); parity is proven (codex byte twins, helper md5 c767f927efd3f6c507ae159194b4bcf2 in all three hand-maintained trees, every call-site hunk mirrored, validate-script-sync OK, edition-sync parity); no secrets and no new trust of operator-controlled input were introduced; 38/38 own probes and the re-run shipped suites (485 + 105 assertions, six targeted walkthrough scenarios) are green — zero admitted findings.
